title: Stanley–Stembridge Conjecture
statement_text: Unit interval graphs have chromatic symmetric functions that expand positively in the $e$-basis of the ring $\mathrm{Sym}$ of symmetric functions.
context_text: Proposed by Stanley and Stembridge in 1993 and reformulated by Guay-Paquet in 2013, this conjecture has driven a large body of research on the chromatic symmetric function $X_G$. The paper shows that the conjecture does not have a K-theoretic lift: the Kromatic symmetric functions $\overline{X}_{P_n}$ of path graphs are not positive in either of two proposed K-analogues of the $e$-basis.
paper_contribution: Demonstrates that the Stanley–Stembridge conjecture does not lift to K-theory, as $\overline{X}_{P_3}$ is not positive in either of two natural K-theoretic deformations of the $e$-basis, suggesting the conjecture is unlikely to be amenable to a topological/Schubert-calculus approach.
paper_title: The Kromatic Symmetric Function: A $K$-theoretic Analogue of $X_G$

title: Grassmannian Subvariety Construction Problem
statement_text: For each claw-free incomparability graph $G$, there should exist a subvariety of the Grassmannian whose cohomology class is represented by $X_G$ and whose K-theoretic structure sheaf class is represented by $\overline{X}_G$. It would be very interesting to have an explicit construction of such subvarieties.
context_text: The authors lift Gasharov's theorem to K-theory by showing that the Kromatic symmetric function of a claw-free incomparability graph is $\overline{s}$-positive. The existence of both a cohomological and a K-theoretic representative suggests a geometric interpretation in terms of Grassmannian subvarieties.
notes: Stated as 'there should be' and 'it would be very interesting'; no labelled theorem environment. PDF source — overline notation for $\overline{X}_G$ may be garbled in extraction.
paper_title: The Kromatic Symmetric Function: A $K$-theoretic Analogue of $X_G$
